column1: # compare.tsv — the `cmp` three-way comparison, exhaustive over scalars.
# `a cmp b` yields -1 / 0 / 1 — a sorts before / with / after b, the
# same total order as lt / gt / sort (see eng/go/compare.go). The rows
# are the full cross product of a representative value of each scalar
# kind, a path value, and every scalar type literal.
# Cross-type order follows the unified lattice Rank and is fully
# transitive. One anomaly is visible: a scalar TYPE LITERAL compared
# within its own family reports the family's zero value — 0 for
# false for Boolean — because the family Comparer is value-based and a
# type literal has no payload to read. So same-family type literals
# compare equal to each other (and to a zero-valued value like '').
# The Rank is not consulted for these: the LCA Comparer settles the
# pair before the Rank fallback is reached.
